exemplar.title:
How does varying the initial angular displacement of a magnetic pendulum affect
its stopping time due to damping caused by the eddy currents induced in the copper
plates below it when the string used for the pendulum, the magnet used, the
dimensions of the copper plate, and the magnetic field strength of the magnet are
kept controlled?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does varying the initial angular displacement of a magnetic pendulum affect
its stopping time due to damping caused by the eddy currents induced in the copper
plates below it when the string used for the pendulum, the magnet used, the
dimensions of the copper plate, and the magnetic field strength of the magnet are
kept controlled?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and focused title and introduction that frame the investigation effectively
Explicit research question tied directly to discussion
Generally appropriate methodology with informed selection of apparatus
weaknesses: Minor lapses in clarity (typos, verbose phrasing) detract occasionally
Treatment of uncertainty and methodological justification is incomplete
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs topic is communicated with sufficient clarity and purpose (Band 3â4), and the research question is clearly stated and connected to the essay. The methodology is mostly complete with relevant sources and equipment, though certain choices (manual timing, limited error analysis) reduce completeness. Taken together, the evidence fits the upper half of the 3â4 band, meriting 4/6.

strengths: Sound application of core physics concepts (eddy currents, damping coefficients)
Generally correct use of key terminology
weaknesses: Occasional symbol and terminology inconsistencies (e.g. Wâ vs Ïâ, âedgy currentâ typo)
Units and dimensional analysis not always explicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good subject knowledge, applying theory to derive models and interpret results, but simplifications and typographical/notation errors prevent the work from reaching the highest band. Terminology is mostly accurate, but minor inaccuracies and omissions (units, consistent symbols) place the work solidly in the mid-band (3/6).

strengths: Data analysis clearly linked to the research question with comparative theoretical vs. experimental graphs
Logical and coherent argument development leading to a conclusion
Awareness of limitations demonstrated through strengths/weaknesses evaluation
weaknesses: Error treatment is superficialâuncertainty not fully propagated or quantified
Some claims (e.g. RÂ² = 1) are accepted without critical scrutiny
Evaluation points tend to be descriptive rather than deeply analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student delivers good research analysis that is relevant to the question, and the argument is well structured (Band 4â6). Evaluation shows a critical perspective, though it lacks depth in quantification of uncertainties. Overall performance fits the mid-band, justifying 5/12.

strengths: Report follows expected structure (title page, contents, sections)
Figures and tables are numbered and word count is within limits
weaknesses: Several low-resolution images and hard-to-read labels
Inconsistent scaling of error bars and some mis-numbered sections
Basic layout supports communication only to a limited extent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure and formatting are generally appropriate, but image quality, label legibility, and inconsistent visual elements restrict clarity. The layout supports basic communication (Band 1â2), earning 2/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Key physics termsââeddy currentsâ, âdamping coefficientâ, âunderdampedââare generally used correctly and consistently.  A few inaccuracies (confusing average time with velocity in a sample calc, mis-typed symbols) reduce precision.  Terminology is therefore mostly, but not fully, accurate, meriting the mid-band score of 3.

The discussion follows a logical sequenceâmethod, results, analysis, evaluationâand arguments are generally coherent, culminating in a conclusion that answers the RQ.  However, reasoning is occasionally repetitive, and some claims (e.g. âRÂ² = 1â for theory) are accepted uncritically.  Structural clarity is present, but the sophistication and depth required for the 7-9 band are lacking, so a low-to-mid score of 4 in the 4-6 band is appropriate.

A workable experimental method, complete variable table, detailed apparatus list and photographs are supplied, showing informed selection of equipment.  However, several methodological choices (manual stopwatch timing, limited amplitude range, no objective stop criterion) weaken completeness, and the treatment of uncertainty is minimal.  The range of sources consulted is mainly textbook/web articles with limited integration into design decisions.  This places the work solidly in the 3-4 band but nearer the lower end (score 3).

The student demonstrates sound understanding of eddy currents, damping coefficients and simple-harmonic-motion relations, applying these ideas to derive a proportional model and to interpret results.  Calculations of Ï, Tâ, and a linear fit between Î¸ and stopping time show appropriate, though not exceptional, use of physics.  Occasional oversimplifications (e.g. constant damping assumed) prevent the work from reaching the sophistication expected for the 5-6 band.

The âEvaluationâ section lists numerous strengths and weaknesses, touching on measurement error, range limitations and theoretical assumptions.  While these points show awareness of shortcomings, many are descriptive rather than deeply analytical (e.g. no quantified impact of pivot friction).  Nonetheless, some critical perspective is evident, meeting the descriptor for basic-to-good evaluation; a score of 4 suits this level.

The student states a concise title and an introduction that immediately frames the investigation around eddy-current damping of a magnetic pendulum.  The purpose, context and basic physical principles are conveyed with generally clear, correct language, enabling the reader to understand what the study is about and why it matters.  Minor lapses such as spelling (âTittleâ) and some verbose phrasing occasionally detract from clarity, but overall the topic remains intelligible and purposeful, warranting the upper end of the 3-4 band rather than the 5-6 band, where communication would need to be consistently precise and polished.

Graphs, tables and photographs are present and labelled; however, several images are low resolution, axes labels are sometimes small, and horizontal error bars are said to be âtoo small to be seenââsignalling inadequate scaling.  The layout therefore supports communication only to a basic extent, matching the 1-2 band; the clearer aspects justify the upper score of 2.

The research question is explicitly provided, identifiable and tied directly to the subsequent discussion.  Although long-winded, it specifies the independent variable (initial angular displacement) and the controlled conditions, giving a reasonably focused direction for the inquiry.  Some breadth remainsâe.g. âstopping timeâ is not rigorously defined (number of oscillations? angle threshold?)âso the focus is not perfect, but it is adequate and clearly connected to the essay, aligning with the 3-4 descriptor.

The essay includes processed data, two comparative graphs (experimental vs theoretical) and some uncertainty bars.  Analysis links trends to energy considerations and magnetic damping theory in a manner clearly relevant to the RQ.  While treatment of error propagation is limited, the student does compare theory and experiment and calculates a gradient that is meaningfully interpreted.  This reflects a good, though not excellent, level of research analysis within the 4-6 band; a mid-score of 5 is justified.

The report follows the prescribed EE structure with title page, contents, labelled sections and subsections.  Figures and tables are numbered, word count is within limits, and overall formatting aids readability.  Minor inconsistencies (occasional mis-numbered section, long paragraphs) keep it from the very top of the band, but the work clearly exceeds the âgenerally appropriateâ level and earns 3 points in the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The spelling of âTittleâ should be corrected to âTitleâ for clarity and professionalism.
The research question is lengthy and âstopping timeâ is not rigorously defined. Specify your stop criterion (e.g. angle threshold or number of oscillations).
Including the word count here complies with IB requirements and confirms the report stays within the 4 000âword limit.
Figure 1 appears low in resolution and the axes labels are difficult to read. Improve image quality and enlarge labels to support clear communication.
The term âedgy currentâ is a typo; it should read âeddy current.â Correct this to maintain conceptual precision.
Equation 1 introduces k_d and v but doesnât label the minus signâs physical meaning. Clarify why F_d is negative (opposes motion).
In Equation 2, include units alongside each term (e.g. Ï in rad/s) to reinforce correct dimensional analysis.
The introduction succinctly describes electromagnetic induction and damping. To deepen subject understanding, link this directly to the pendulumâs energy decay model.
The raw data table omits explicit uncertainty columns for time and angle. Include these to support later error analysis.
The assumption of constant damping oversimplifies reality. Consider modelling Î³(v) as velocityâdependent or segmenting data to account for changing damping.
The concluding reflection merely restates strengths and weaknesses. Add insight on how these will inform future planning and method improvements.
Claiming RÂ²=1 without acknowledging realâworld losses is uncritical. Discuss why a perfect fit is unlikely in an experimental context.
Including video analysis to confirm underdamped behavior shows strong initiative and deepens understanding through multiple methods.
The negative value for Î³ contradicts physical expectations (Î³>0). Reâevaluate your logarithmic calculation or sign convention.
References in the conclusion are informal. Apply a consistent citation style (MLA/APA/Chicago) and include inâtext citations where appropriate.
When stating the undamped angular frequency, remind the reader that the smallâangle approximation was assumed (sinâÎ¸âÎ¸).
The symbol âW_0â is introduced but inconsistent with earlier Ï notation. Use consistent symbols (e.g. Ïâ) throughout your derivation.
You assume k_dâTâ without justification. Provide a physical rationale or reference for linking the damping constant to the natural period.
The variables table lists controls inconsistently (split lines, missing details). Consolidate each controlled variable and its control method in one row.
Manual stopwatch timing introduces significant random error. Consider using a highâspeed camera or motion sensor for more precise stoppingâtime measurement.
The sample calculation formula incorrectly multiplies the sum of times by 5. It should be average = (sum of trials)/7. Correct the algebra to avoid error.
Stating that error bars are âtoo small to be seenâ is insufficient. Provide a scale of uncertainty or zoomedâin inset to visually represent horizontal errors.
The theoretical stoppingâtime values are presented without derivation. Show key calculation steps or reference your equation explicitly for transparency.
The strengths listed in the evaluation are descriptive rather than analytical. Quantify the impact of each strength on your results to deepen critical evaluation.
The weakness section is wordy and overlaps points. Condense each assumptionâs description and relate directly to how it could skew your damping coefficient.
